title: Desktop Local Data Adapter
description: Electron desktop host concern — **Desktop Local Data Adapter**. Local mode runs `createKernel` in the main process and exposes IPC-backed ports; remote mode reuses the HTTP client stack. Renderer-side `SpecdDataPort` that calls IPC instead of HTTP for the local desktop profile.
context: Electron desktop host concern — **Desktop Local Data Adapter**. Local mode runs `createKernel` in the main process and exposes IPC-backed ports; remote mode reuses the HTTP client stack. Renderer-side `SpecdDataPort` that calls IPC instead of HTTP for the local desktop profile.

rules.requirement: local adapter is selected for opened project folders
rules: When the user opens a local project in desktop, the renderer MUST use this adapter instead of `adapter-remote-specd-data`.

rules.requirement: local adapter does not attach Authorization headers
rules: The local profile MUST NOT use `client:adapter-bearer-auth`.

scenarios.requirement: local adapter is selected for opened project folders
scenarios.name: Open folder switches renderer to IPC adapter
when: user opens local project via welcome
then: AND remote HTTP adapter not active

scenarios.requirement: local adapter is selected for opened project folders
scenarios.name: Remote connect keeps HTTP adapter
then: AND IPC adapter not used for data

scenarios.requirement: local adapter is selected for opened project folders
scenarios.name: Profile switch swaps adapter instance
when: user moves from local to remote profile

scenarios.requirement: local adapter does not attach Authorization headers
scenarios.name: IPC invoke has no Authorization metadata
then: IPC envelope has no bearer field
AND main process uses kernel actor resolver

scenarios.requirement: local adapter does not attach Authorization headers
scenarios.name: Accidental token in local profile is ignored
given: stale token stored from remote session
then: AND token not sent to main

scenarios.requirement: local adapter does not attach Authorization headers
scenarios.name: Remote adapter still attaches bearer when configured
then: AND local and remote paths differ by design
